trails.name: Castle rock - Bridle path - Goats track
Lord of The Possums MTB Track
Major Hornbrook Track
trails.description: Enjoy sweeping views back down into Lyttelton Harbour and across to Banks Peninsula and a good day you can see Canterbury Plains to the Kaikoura ranges to the north.

trails.name: Day walk
trails.description: McLeans walking track

trails.name: Short walk
trails.description: Taranaki walkway entrance at carpark downstream from kings ave

trails.name: Short walk
trails.description: Poynter Nature walk, eastern part

trails.name: Combined mtb and walk
trails.description: Kaiapoi Island Trail from Waiora Stream to start of Forest Route and River Route

trails.name: Combined mtb and walk
trails.description: shared walking and biking track at Smith Stream

trails.name: Combined mtb and walk
trails.description: Kaiapoi Island Trail,from Raven Quay to Waiora Stream, on stopbank

trails.name: Combined mtb and walk
trails.description: rakahuri trail just off kings ave on the riverside of the stop bank.

trails.name: Harts Creek Track
trails.description: This is an easy track that follows Harts Creek. Wildlife viewing opportunities make this a great walk for children and families.

trails.name: Major Hornbrook Track
trails.description: Enjoy sweeping views back down into Lyttelton Harbour and across to Banks Peninsula and a good day you can see Canterbury Plains to the Kaikoura ranges to the north.

trails.name: Birdlings Flat To Little River Track
Mt Oxford Track
trails.description: Mt Oxford Track takes you from Coopers Creek car park to the summit of Mt Oxford with good views of the Canterbury Plains and into Lees Valley.

trails.name: Youngman Stream Track
trails.description: Follows Ashley River to Youngman Stream Hut.

trails.name: Pinchgut Track
trails.description: This track is a challenging day or multi-day hike. It is suitable for trampers with a moderate to high level of backcountry experience. Follow the Okuku River to Pinchgut Hut.

trails.name: Summit WWay  Mt Herbert to Port Levy
View Hill to Mt Oxford Track
trails.description: Follow the ridge through forest and tussock to get to the summit of Mt Oxford with good views of the Canterbury Plains and into Lees Valley.

trails.name: Wharfedale Track to Mt Oxford Route
Bypass Track
trails.description: This track links Richardson and Blowhard tracks.

trails.name: Summit Track
trails.description: Summit Track follows steep ridge to the summit of Mt Thomas.

trails.name: Ridge Track
trails.description: Enjoy sweeping panoramic views of the Canterbury Plains, Banks Peninsula and the Southern Alps/Kā Tiritiri o te Moana from Mt Thomas (1023 m).

trails.name: Red Beech Track
trails.description: Combine this track with the Mt Grey/Maukatere Track to form a circuit of Mt Grey/Maukatere. The track is steep and easiest to descent than climb.

trails.name: Kaituna Valley Packhorse Hut Track
trails.description: The walk to Packhorse Hut can be enjoyed as a half day walk but is also a great overnight tramp for families with children and new trampers.

trails.name: View Hill to Coopers Creek Link Track
Awaroa/Godley Head Loop Track
trails.description: This dramatic headland sits on top of sheer 120 metre-high cliffs. It offers spectacular views and the chance to explore the historic Godley Head coastal defence battery and Scott's Cabin.

trails.name: Black Hill Hut tracks
trails.description: Walk to Black Hill Hut on one of these tracks or combine them to make a circuit.

trails.name: Bob’s Camp Bivouac Route
trails.description: Visit a cute bivvy with an unusual curved roof.

trails.name: Christchurch to Little River Rail Trail
trails.description: The whole 46 km-long walkway and cycleway runs from Hornby in Christchurch to Little River on Banks Peninsula. The Motukarara to Little River section described here covers 20 km.

trails.name: Glentui Loop Track
trails.description: This family walk starts from the pleasant Glentui picnic area.

trails.name: Mt Grey/Maukatere tracks
trails.description: Enjoy some of the best views in Canterbury from the summit of Mt Grey/Maukatere (934 m).

trails.name: Mt Oxford tracks
trails.description: Climb to the summit of Mt Oxford (1364 m) for good views of the Canterbury Plains and into Lees Valley.

trails.name: Mt Richardson tracks
trails.description: Climb to the summit of Mt Richardson (1047 m) for great views to the Puketeraki Range and over Lees Valley.

trails.name: Mt Thomas tracks
trails.description: Enjoy sweeping panoramic views of the Canterbury Plains, Banks Peninsula and the Southern Alps/Kā Tiritiri o te Moana from Mt Thomas (1023 m).

trails.name: Ōtamahua/Quail Island tracks
trails.description: The beautiful and pest free Ōtamahua is home to historic sites such as the ship’s graveyard and the quarantine barracks. It has swimming and waterskiing beaches and a bird-watching barricade.

trails.name: Ōtukaikino Walk
trails.description: This short, easy track loops around a wetland. Interpretive panels provide information about the wetland and it's inhabitants.

trails.name: Ryde Falls tracks
trails.description: Walk to the five-tier Ryde Falls.

trails.name: Tarn Hut tracks
trails.description: These tracks can be combined to form a circuit walk to Tarn Hut.

trails.name: Wharfedale Hut tracks
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike to Wharfedale Hut.

trails.name: Wooded Gully loop walks
trails.description: There are numerous options, some suitable for children, for a loop walk from Wooded Gully picnic area.

trails.name: Hay Scenic Reserve walk
trails.description: This walk through stunning lowland podocarp forest is suitable for young children and family groups.

trails.name: Kaituna Valley Scenic Reserve walk
trails.description: This easy forest walk is ideal for families with young children. Starting and finishing at the picnic area, it winds through the trees of the reserve and surrounding grassy areas.

trails.name: Nīkau Palm Gully Scenic Reserve walk
trails.description: Walk to a small forest pocket of nīkau palms and enjoy views of the Akaroa Marine Reserve. The walk crosses private land and is closed from 1 April to 30 September. Respect the seasonal closures.

trails.name: Tutakakahikura Scenic Reserve walk
trails.description: You need a 4WD vehicle to get to the start of the walk to Tutakakahikura Scenic Reserve. This is a narrow stand of remnant red beech/tawairaunui forest.

trails.name: Mount Herbert Walkway/Te Ahu Patiki
trails.description: Take a day tramp and enjoy spectacular views of Banks Peninsula and the Canterbury Plains from the Mount Herbert/Te Ahu Patiki summit.

trails.name: Saddle Hill Summit Track
trails.description: This family-friendly walk has stunning views of Akaroa Harbour, Kaitorete, and the peaks of Banks Peninsula to the South Alps.

trails.name: Awaroa/Godley Head Loop Track
trails.description: This dramatic headland sits on top of sheer 120 metre-high cliffs. It offers spectacular views and the chance to explore the historic Godley Head coastal defence battery and Scott's Cabin.

trails.name: Mount Pleasant Track
trails.description: Enjoy panoramic views along the tops of the Mount Pleasant bluffs on this great summer walk, which includes historic gun emplacements.

trails.name: Blue Track
trails.description: The track begins 200 m from Bottle Lake Forest car park and winds around the forest and scrublands until finishing in the carpark. There are a number of walks that can be made using dedicated walking tracks and shared use roads that are set out in a grid pattern for wayfinding.

trails.name: Botanic Gardens River Walk
trails.description: There is an extensive network of paths to explore through the Botanic Gardens. This walk is recommended for those wanting a leisurely and scenic stroll from Rolleston Ave to the Armagh Street carpark.

trails.name: Bowenvale Valley walk
trails.description: A  great walk with plenty of variety and sweeping views over the Canterbury Plains. The trail starts as a shared vehicle track until further up the valley where it winds up through open tussock.

trails.name: Brooklands Lagoon Track
trails.description: Begin at the observation platform looking out over Brooklands Lagoon. The track follows the lagoon to a second observation platform and then continues around the estuary edge to a well signposted track junction. Return the same way.

trails.name: Canterbury Wetland Walk
trails.description: Start from the Visitor Centre, cross the road and take the path over the wooden bridge. From here you can enjoy walking the various paths that meander through the plantings and around the wetland. The Halswell Quarry Conservation Area was established in 1999 creating wetland areas and extensive native planting that is now home to a variety of birdlife.

trails.name: Captain Thomas Track
trails.description: From Sumnervale Reserve follow the track markers to a stile which takes you to Greenwood Park. The track climbs uphill beside a small stream before climbing through a section of switch backs and through rocky terrain to Evans Pass Road. Cross into Rapanui Bush where a short track will take you to Evans Pass where you can enjoy views across Lyttelton Harbour. You can return the same way or via the Scarborough Bluffs Track back to Sumner.

trails.name: Cashmere Hill Loop
trails.description: This short walking circuit provides a good workout for those with limited time, as well as great views over the park.

trails.name: Cedrics Track
trails.description: Follow the track across open tussock grassland until arriving at Thomsons carpark. Cross the carpark and head down the track and over a stile. Climb steadily down the Plains side of Sugarloaf and follow the track until reaching Sign of the Kiwi. Create a loop using Gilpins or Mitchells Tracks.

trails.name: Christchurch Coastal Path
trails.description: Follow the new shared use path from Scott Park in Ferrymead along the coast to Redcliffs. Follow the footpath along Beachville Road around to Redcliffs Village and then take a narrow footway around Moncks Bay and through to Shag Rock.

trails.name: City Promenade
trails.description: Oxford Terrace has become a broad, paved area for pedestrians and cyclists. With native plantings and riverside beauty to one side, and cafes, bars, attractions to the other, this walk is the perfect way to get to know Christchurch’s city centre a little better. The promenade finishes at the other end of Oxford Terrace, past the Margaret Mahy playground.

trails.name: Coastal Cliffs Walkway
trails.description: The Coastal Cliffs Walkway follows the foreshore between Church Bay, Diamond Harbour and Purau, with harbour views. It forms part of the Head to Head Walkway.

trails.name: Coastal Path Lyttelton
trails.description: This wide, well-formed track follows the coastline well above the shore. Stop to visit Magazine, Corsair and Cass Bays along the way.

trails.name: Coronation Hill
trails.description: Follow the track as it skirts around scrubby slopes high above Lyttelton Harbour before turning back to the Summit Road near a large gravel car park. Complete the circuit and return to the Sign of the Kiwi by picking up the track to the left that sidles above the Summit Road and ends near the cattle stop by the Sign of the Kiwi car park.

trails.name: Drayton Reserve Track
trails.description: Enter the reserve from near the first hairpin bend on Glenstrae Road. The track rises gently through the reserve with many additional branch tracks connecting to nearby roads and properties. A community group works to re-forest Drayton - look out for new plantings and the native birds who enjoy them.

trails.name: Eastenders Track
trails.description: From Barnett Park, walk across the sports field towards the head of the valley. A small bridge crosses a creek and the track starts to climb up the eastern flank. Here you leave the old track and follow the new one which branches off uphill. It follows a number of twists and turns to link up with a 4WD track and finishes at the stand of large pine trees on Summit Road.

trails.name: Eastside Bush Track
trails.description: Explore the Eastside Bush Track that descends into Bowenvale Valley, passing through a mixture of native and exotic forest.

trails.name: Ernle Clark Reserve Walk
trails.description: Follow the formed gravel path beside the river through the beautiful woodland reserve Ernle Clark. For a longer walk, continue along the sealed path by the river and cross Cashmere Road to reach Cashemere Valley Reserve (30 minutes return).

trails.name: Estuary Edge Walk
trails.description: Follow the track to where the new boardwalk begins. The boardwalk takes you over the salt marshes where wetland birds can be spotted and lovely views of the mountains and water surround.

trails.name: Gilpins Track
trails.description: Follow Cedrics Track over gentle bedrock before reaching a junction. Take a left onto Gilpins Track and sidle through native bush before descending via a number of switch backs in open tussock grassland. Finish on Dyers Pass Road opposite Sign of the Kiwi. Create a loop using Cedrics of Mitchells Tracks.

trails.name: Godley Walkway
trails.description: The track hugs the coastline, offering views back to Whitewash Head and across Pegasus Bay to the Kaikoura Ranges. Keep an eye out for wildlife along the way.  There are a variety of seabirds, including white-flippered penguins nesting along this coastline. If you are lucky you may spot a pod of rare Hector's dolphins, some seals or even a whale.

trails.name: Governors Bay Foreshore Track
trails.description: This track starts beside the Governors Bay jetty at the end of Jetty Road. It's a gentle stroll around the harbour foreshore along a wide, gravelled track, suitable for all ages and fitness levels. No beaches here but it's good for spotting birdlife on the mudflats.

trails.name: Green Track
trails.description: The track begins 200 m from Bottle Lake Forest carpark and takes a shorter route through the forest before finishing in the carpark.

trails.name: Hagley Park River Walk
trails.description: A dog and cycle friendly alternative to the Botanic Gardens River Walk, this trail follows the Avon around the Gardens on the Hagley Park bank. See the Kate Sheppard Memorial Walk bursting with camellias in late winter, the impressive collection within the pinetum and the gorgeous Woodland Garden full of primula and iris. Walk back up Rolleston Ave and turn into the car park to finish.

trails.name: Harry Ell Walkway
trails.description: From Sign of the Takahe, head up the well maintained track and gradually climb through a mixture of native and exotic woodland before emerging on Summit Road.

trails.name: Hays Bay to Church Bay
trails.description: The track meanders above the foreshore and boat sheds before reaching the beach at Hays Bay. Cross the beach to the steps on the far side and climb up to Black Point Rd.

trails.name: Heathcote River Trail
trails.description: Follow the Heathcote River from the base of the Port Hills all the way out to the sea. The river meanders through numerous parks and reserves making it an attractive walk or ride through the eastern city. Create a walk out of your favourite section or set a challenge to complete the entire length. Many sections are shared use so watch out for cyclists who may be sharing the path with you.

trails.name: Heathcote Towpath
trails.description: Walk along the river and spot a number of sites associated with early settler history, including where early vessels used to dock and the site of the first European ferry.

trails.name: Hidden Valley Track
trails.description: The track heads up a side valley and sidles along open slopes before descending through pine trees and regenerating bush then exiting near a wooden bridge.

trails.name: Holdsworth Track
trails.description: At the junction near the top of Kennedys Bush Track, bear right to Kennedys Bush Scenic Reserve and pick up Holdsworth Track from here. Holdsworth Track links Kennedys Bush Track with Sign of the Bellbird and the Orongomai Trail.

trails.name: Horseshoe Perimeter Walk
trails.description: Follow the path as it curves around the perimeter of Horseshoe Lake. The path becomes a track once off Lake Terrace Road and finishes about 500m down Horseshoe Lake Road.

trails.name: Huntsbury Reserve Track
trails.description: Wind through bush to connect with the network of tracks in the Mt Vernon Park.

trails.name: Hussey View Walkway
trails.description: Styx Mill Conservation Reserve, off Hussey View Road

trails.name: Island River Track
trails.description: This interesting, winding trail follows the Otukaikino River through native bush and scrub before circling a small island and returning the same way.

trails.name: Kennedy's Bush Access Track
trails.description: From Findlay's picnic area, follow the track uphill through the gum trees and the sheep paddocks until you reach a zig zag section that comes out onto Kennedys Bush Road.

trails.name: Kenton Track
trails.description: Follow the uphill track into John Britten Reserve and enjoy expansive views of the Heathcote Valley and back over the estuary.

trails.name: L'Aube Hill Reserve and Settlers Hill Walk
trails.description: This bush clad track visits the oldest cemetery in the South Island. The first section is short and steep and goes through bush - turn right at the junction to visit the cemetery. Return to the junction and turn right again to follow the bush track to Libeau Lane. Clime the stile and go down the lane to Rue Grehan. This street has many charming historic cottages. Turn left onto Rue Lavaud, cut through the church and then go through a gate to return where you started.

trails.name: Lake Circuit Walk
trails.description: Visit the beautiful Roto Kohatu Reserve and enjoy a relaxing and easy stroll around the sparkling Lakes Tahi and Rua.

trails.name: Latters Spur Track
trails.description: This walk has lots of variety, passing through a mixture of exotic and regenerating native forest. It heads up past the downhill mountain bike tracks and jumps at Victoria Park and finishes with a stunning view over the Canterbury Plains from Sugarloaf Reserve.

trails.name: Little Hagley Park Walk
trails.description: Follow the meandering river downstream and enjoy a scenery that changes dramatically depending on the season. Head over to Hagley Park North for a longer walk or create a loop with the pretty Millbrook Reserve Walk.

trails.name: Major Hornbrook
trails.description: A downhill track to Lyttelon, head under rocky bluffs before descending through a field of boulders and regenerating bush in Major Hornbrook Reserve. Continue on until the track joins Ross Terrace. Turn left onto Ross Terrace and continue across Cornwall Road. Turn right at Canterbury Road which will take you straight down to London Street.

trails.name: Maori Garden
trails.description: From the main road the track winds down the hill with steps and steep sections to the rocky area above the secluded beach. The track follows the cliff edge to Sandy Bay. Take care - the track has some uneven surfaces and slippery sections.

trails.name: Matuku Lakes Loop Track
trails.description: Meander around the Matuku Lakes on this gentle loop walk that utilises the various paths in Ferrymead Park. The lakes were originally designed as a flood retention pond and aspirationally named for the Australasian bittern, or Matuku, which hopefully will return one day to Christchurch's wetlands.

trails.name: Mitchells Track
trails.description: Follow the track through native bush and sidle around steep rocky bluffs until reaching a spur. Head down into a section of pretty native bush before finishing opposite Sign of the Kiwi. Create a loop using Gilpins or Cedrics Tracks.

trails.name: Mona Vale
trails.description: The beautiful heritage gardens and buildings provide a lovely atmosphere for a peaceful stroll. Walk along the river and enjoy the colourful annual bedding and woodland plantings. The rose and iris gardens are similarly gorgeous spots for a meander and expansive lawns have multiple places for picnics.

trails.name: Nga Puna Wai Loop Walk
trails.description: This walk takes you on the outskirts of the native wetland plants around the Nga Puna Wai lake. You can start in either direction. There is a small boardwalk on the western side of the loop.

trails.name: Nga Puna Wai Walk
trails.description: From the car park off Augustine Drive turn right and follow the path that leads into the small native forest. There are several offshoots that you can use to turn the track into a loop. The second half of the walk takes you through open fields and onto a shared cycle path along Curletts Road.

trails.name: Ngaio Point Coastal Track
trails.description: Start by going through a gate near the layby on Monarch Drive. Follow the track downhill and continue around Ngaio Point headland. The track leads to Bayview Beach, cross the beach and climb the steps up to Bayview Cresent. Turn left and follow the road to Upper Bayview Reserve, from here the track leads you through the reserve to SH75. Cross the stile and walk carefully along the road back to Monarch Drive.

trails.name: North Hagley Park and Gardens Loop
trails.description: Follow the path north around the perimeter of Hagley Park. Turn onto Riccarton Ave and head along the path the Gardens. In spring the woodland is full of daffodils. The Bandsmen's Memorial Rotunda and Heritage Rose Garden are well worth a short detour. Cross the Woodland Bridge into the rest of the Botanic Gardens. Stop to see the Peace Bell and enjoy the Central Rose Garden and conservatories before taking the Armagh Street footbridge back to the car park where you started.

trails.name: North Hagley Park Walk
trails.description: Follow the path north of Victoria Lake to the Armagh St Bridge. Head north along Park Terrace. Skirt around the park bordering Harper Avenue when in spring the path becomes a boulevard of Yoshino cherry blossom and blooming bluebells in Little Hagley Park. Continue along Deans Avenue before heading east, taking the walkway beneath the Oriental plane trees back to Victoria Lake.

trails.name: Opawaho River Track
trails.description: This track follows the ?p?waho/Heathcote estuary edge through Ferrymead Park, passing an important wildlife area managed by Forest and Bird.

trails.name: Orongomai Trail
trails.description: Cross the stile and follow the track as it zigzags down through bush past several track junctions. The track eventually becomes rougher and descends to a small stream. The lower part of the reserve has some of the best accessible forest in the Port Hills. At a stream confluence the track starts climbing, passing several large totara and matai trees. Continue climbing steadily through kanuka forest and follow the signs back to the Sign of the Bellbird.

trails.name: Otukaikino Track
trails.description: The track is divided into four sections and linear walks can be created around these to suit individual needs. The main entry points are Darroch Street Reserve, The Groynes dog park, Clearwater Resort and Roto Kohatu Reserve

trails.name: Otukaikino Wetland Track
trails.description: Wheelchair/Pram Track

trails.name: Panako Walkway
trails.description: Styx Mill Conservation Reserve, off Styx Mill Road

trails.name: Papanui Stream Walk
trails.description: Follow the meandering Papanui Stream through the neighbourhood for a peaceful walk through urban green space. Children will enjoy Erica Park playground on the way through.

trails.name: Quarry Rim Track
trails.description: The Quarry Rim Track is suitable for all ages. It starts from the main car park and heads up the hill above the old quarry buildings, showcasing panoramic views of the Southern Alps and the city. Information panels depict the workings and historic features of the quarry and viewing platforms and seating provide ideal resting points along the route. The track descends to Findlay's picnic area and back to the main car park.

trails.name: Quarry Trail
trails.description: Start by taking the Orongomai Trail as above but some way down the descent, turn off to take the Quarry Trail. Almost immediately you will pass under the old cut faces of a small quarry site. Some of the rocks underfoot can be slippery as this is a damp spot. Sidle and then start a gradual climb to meet the Oronogomai Trail. Continue back to the Sign of the Bellbird.

trails.name: Sandy Bay to Jetty Road Track
trails.description: This undulating single track follows the water edge through coastal bush before climbing some stairs to reach Jetty Road.

trails.name: Scarborough Bluffs Track
trails.description: A gentle descent through open tussock. The terrain becomes quite rocky as it weaves through the bluffs and past the Scarborough Woolshed. Gently sidles down a slope to meet Taylors Mistake Road. Try making a loop with the Captain Thomas Track.

trails.name: Sister City Gardens Walk
trails.description: A choice of easy, accessible walks connect small botanical collections representing six of Christchurch's sister cities: Adelaide (South Australia), Christchurch (England), Gansu Province (China), Kurashiki (Japan), Seattle (USA), and Songpa-Gu (Korea). The landscaping of the gardens was carefully planned with each of the Sister Cities. Each botanical collection has diverse native plant types from around each Sister City region.

trails.name: South Hagley Park Walk
trails.description: Follow the path northwest towards the Riccarton Road traffic lights and its group of mature oaks and elms. Turn south along Deans Avenue, a good choice if looking for autumn colour, and on to Moorhouse Avenue where spring daffodils flower en masse under deciduous European trees. Continue around to Hospital Corner and back to the car park.

trails.name: Southern Pegasus Bay Track
trails.description: This extensive track spans from Southshore Spit to Spencer Park Surf Club following a trail in the dune crest and winding through forest by Spencer Park and Bottle Lake.  For a shorter walk, start at Broad Park and either head north to the Surf Club or south to the Spit.

trails.name: Southshore Spit Track
trails.description: From Rockinghorse Road walk past the information panel and toilet and start along the Southshore Spit dune loop track. This wanders through the coastal duneland out towards the beach before swinging around to the estuary side of the spit.

trails.name: Spencer Park Wetland Walk
trails.description: Start from the carpark of the main picnic area and walk around the circuit track on a series of boardwalks. Finish back at the carpark.

trails.name: Stan Helms Track
trails.description: An interesting link from Lyttelton back up to Summit Road, the track climbs steadily to a prominent spur then weaves up to connect with the Crater Rim Walkway and the car park at the top of the Bridle Path.

trails.name: Stanley Park
trails.description: A network of informal tracks meander through the park, offering stunning views over the harbour from open grassland areas. The main track starts at Beach Road and heads up to the Beach Road lookout above the fire station. From the lookout, the track curves through kanuka forest before crossing through grasslands and Penlington Place to pick up the track as it ends at Watson Street.

trails.name: Styx River Esplanade Reserve
trails.description: A walking track follows along the river’s edge from the playground to the springs area, past ponds and through native plantings.

trails.name: Sumner Promenade
trails.description: Start from Cave Rock and stroll along the wide asphalt path next to the sea. On a summer's day the promenade is busy with other walkers, joggers, parents with strollers or people sitting and enjoying the day.

trails.name: Taste of the Gardens
trails.description: There is an extensive network of paths to explore through the Botanic Gardens. This recommended walk is perfect for visitors with limited time who want a taste of what the Gardens has to offer.

trails.name: Tawhairaunui Track
trails.description: Follow the path through the wooded area with boardwalks and viewing platforms. The second  viewing platform is shaded by a large tawhairaunu (red beech tree). Zig zag up through native plantings to cross Victoria Park Road and head down to finish at the Visitor Centre.

trails.name: Taylor's Mistake Walk
trails.description: Walk from the beach into a dense grove of trees and bush. Once free of the canopy, the track gets gradually steeper as it winds around the cliff. Take in the scenery at the top before taking a zig zag track along the cliffs before ending at Nicholson Park.

trails.name: Te Ara Avon Trail
trails.description: This shared-use trail is a transitional walking and biking track along the Avon/?t?karo River. While the future of the area is still being decided, take the opportunity to explore parts of the Red Zone on this trail.

trails.name: Te Oka Reserve
trails.description: An unformed track over open tussock grassland with a short walk on gravel roads near the bottom of the reserve. The return climb takes approximately 2.5 hours or arrange a car to pick you up from the beach.

trails.name: Travis Wetland Walk
trails.description: A pleasant stroll around the wetlands - use the observation tower and bird hide to spot rare native wetland birds.

trails.name: Waimairi Walkway
trails.description: Follow the track along the river until it crosses the road and brings you out of the Groynes. After a short walk alongside the motorway, the track heads through the countryside until arriving at Darroch Street Reserve. From here, head back the way you came or use the ?tukaikino Track to form a loop.

trails.name: Waimakariri Walkway
trails.description: Begin opposite the beach carpark on Heyders Rd, the trail is wide and well marked with kilometre indicators. The track finishes on the beach at the mouth of the lagoon after passing through a pine forest

trails.name: Wetland Edge Walk
trails.description: From the car park, turn right to reach the start of the wetland walk and follow the well formed gravel path past the edge of tidal pools and regenerating wetland vegetation. Either turn left at the first junction to return to the car park or continue along to make a slightly longer loop around Ti Rakau Reserve.  You can also connect to Ti Rakau Drive, Dyers Road or Waterman Place in Ferrymead through the Reserve.
